claims.evidence_text: The benchmark canopy size at flowering is GAI of 6.3 (fertile shoots 6.0, infertile shoots 0.3).
claims.claim_summary: Benchmark optimum green area index (GAI/LAI) for winter wheat at flowering is about 6.3.

claims.evidence_text: Small canopies (GAI <4), which can result from inadequate shoots or N deficiency, waste sunlight. Large canopies (GAI >7) ... cost more than is necessary to intercept all available sunlight.
claims.claim_summary: Target GAI window for winter wheat is roughly 4-7; below 4 wastes sunlight, above 7 is wasteful and raises disease/lodging risk.

claims.evidence_text: The benchmark rate of canopy expansion is 0.1 GAI/day, hence crops expand by 3 GAI units during May.
claims.claim_summary: Benchmark winter wheat canopy expansion rate is 0.1 GAI units per day during May.

claims.evidence_text: N availability controls canopy expansion quite closely because crop N for each unit of green area remains constant at 36 kg/ha – the canopy N requirement.
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat canopy N requirement is constant at about 36 kg N/ha per unit of green area index.

claims.evidence_text: Light interception is sufficient for rapid growth when GAI ≥3.
claims.claim_summary: Light interception is sufficient for rapid winter wheat growth once GAI reaches about 3.
